title: Lens verdict output (canonical)
description: Canonical two-phase (deterministic then semantic) verdict shape every Lens / lens-fast validation returns. Deterministic must be fully populated and passing before any semantic finding lands. See ../SKILL.md for the phase ordering and evidence rules this schema encodes.

verdict.description: Even one FAIL in deterministic or semantic collapses the whole verdict to FAIL.

lens_tier.description: Depth classification this dispatch ran at (see SKILL.md 3-tier table). T0 dispatches typically omit a row entirely rather than emitting this schema.

deterministic.description: REQUIRED before any semantic finding lands. If any entry's exit_code != 0, verdict is FAIL and the response returns immediately — no semantic review on a failing build.

status.description: not_configured is reported explicitly (never silently skipped) and does NOT degrade the verdict to FAIL by itself.

semantic.description: Only populated if every deterministic gate passed. Rotate SECURITY / NEW-HIRE / OPS perspectives.

conflicts.description: Spec-vs-implementation disagreements. The orchestrator decides which side to update — Lens must never silently pick the implementation side.

evidence.description: file:line, test name, OR verbatim command output the orchestrator can verify itself. Paraphrase ('I checked X') is not evidence.

open_questions.description: Theoretical worst-cases that are not data-loss/security/contract violations land here, not as a FAIL.

stdout.description: Verbatim captured output. Unexpected warnings/deprecations/flaky retries count as FAIL — never paper over them.

fix_hint.description: Optional. A hint, never code — Lens cannot write code (disallowedTools: Edit, Write, NotebookEdit).
